item.title: Japan shifts focus to oil in unorthodox scramble to talk up yen
item.description: Japan is weighing a controversial plan to arrest the yen's slide: stepping into oil futures markets, sources say, as long-standing policy tools lose traction вҖӢagainst stubborn inflation pressures.

item.title: Revolut to base 40% of its global workforce in India by 2026
item.description: European fintech firm Revolut plans to have around 40% of its global workforce based in India by the end of 2026 as вҖӢit expands its India global capability centre.

item.title: Trade ministers meet in Cameroon for critical WTO reform talks amid deep divisions
item.description: Trade ministers will meet in Cameroon on Thursday for crucial discussions on reforming the World Trade Organization, as some diplomats and trade officials warn that вҖӢwithout an agreement, countries may set trade rules outside the organisation.

item.title: Nvidia-backed Reflection AI eyes $25 billion valuation, WSJ reports
item.description: Nvidia-backed startup Reflection AI is in talks to вҖӢraise $2.5 billion at a вҖҢvaluation of $25 billion, the Wall Street Journal reported on Wednesday, вҖӢciting people familiar with вҖӢthe matter.

item.title: Stocks on edge as Middle East ceasefire talks take centre stage
item.description: Asian stocks struggled for direction while the dollar held firm on Thursday as investors treaded вҖӢcautiously amid dizzying developments in the Middle East, where Iran said it would weigh a U.S. proposal to вҖҢend the Gulf conflict.

item.title: Nanya Technology shares surge 10% after $2.5 billion private placement
item.description: Shares of вҖӢmemory chip вҖҢmaker Nanya Technology opened вҖӢon вҖӢThursday limit up 10% вҒ after вҖӢit issued вҖӢa $2.5 billion private placement backed вҖӢby tech вҖӢfirms such as вҖҢSandisk вҒ Technology.

item.title: Trump's tariffs had little impact on GDP in 2025, but raised revenue, academic paper finds
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump's barrage of tariffs last year had only a minimal impact on U.S. economic output but raised significant federal revenue and вҖӢcontributed to a further U.S.-China trade decoupling, a new Brookings Institution academic вҖҢpaper showed on Wednesday.

item.title: Brookings Institution paper charts path toward smaller Fed balance sheet
item.description: If the Federal Reserve truly wants a smaller balance sheet, it can get there with regulatory changes, tweaks to the payment system and more frequent market interventions by the вҖӢcentral bank, new research published by the Brookings Institution said on Wednesday.

item.title: Worker sues Valero over Texas refinery explosion
item.description: A man who said he was injured in an explosion at Valero Energy's Port Arthur, Texas refinery on Monday night вҖӢfiled a lawsuit on Wednesday in a state district court вҖҢalleging the company failed to properly maintain the refinery.

item.title: Hyundai Motor plans to launch 36 new models in North America by 2030
item.description: Hyundai Motor вҖӢsaid on вҖҢThursday it is targeting вҖӢvehicle вҖӢsales of 500,000 вҒ units in вҖӢChina this вҖӢyear, and plans to launch вҖӢ36 вҖӢnew models in the вҖҢNorth вҒ American market by 2030.

item.title: Japan's corporate service inflation perks up in February
item.description: A key gauge of Japan's service-sector inflation rose 2.7% in February from a вҖӢyear earlier, data showed on Thursday, reinforcing the вҖҢcentral bank's view that a tight labour market is pushing firms to pass rising costs on to consumers.

item.title: Brazil's Americanas files motion to exit bankruptcy protection proceedings
item.description: Brazilian retailer Americanas вҖӢsaid on Wednesday it filed вҖҢa motion in a Rio de Janeiro court to exit bankruptcy protection proceedings, вҖӢas the company claims it вҖӢfulfilled all previously established obligations.

item.title: US oil prices rise as investors assess Middle East de-escalation
item.description: U.S. oil prices rose in early trade on Thursday, recovering some of the вҖӢprevious day's losses as investors assessed prospects вҖҢfor de-escalation in the Middle East and Iran reviewed a U.S. proposal to end the war, which has вҖӢdisrupted energy flows from the Gulf.

item.title: Super Micro sued by shareholders over China-related criminal case against co-founder, others
item.description: Shareholders of Super Micro Computer sued the Silicon Valley server maker on Wednesday, accusing it of committing securities fraud by вҖӢconcealing its dependence on sales to China that violated U.S. вҖҢexport laws, leading to criminal smuggling charges involving Nvidia chips against a co-founder and two others linked to the company.

item.title: JBS posts stable profit in Q4 despite record revenue
item.description: Brazil's JBS , the world's largest meat company, on Wednesday вҖӢreported a nearly flat fourth-quarter net вҖҢprofit as record revenue failed to offset tighter margins, especially in its U.S. beef business.

item.title: Trading Day: Giving peace a chance
item.description: Stocks rose and oil prices and bond yields dropped on Wednesday, on hopes that the U.S. and Iran are progressing toward a peace deal. Investors' optimism was reflected in the MSCI All Country equity index clocking its biggest rise in six weeks.

item.title: Explainer: What comes next after the social media trial verdicts?
item.description: Recent jury verdicts in California and New Mexico involving Meta Platforms and Alphabet's Google are sharpening scrutiny of social media companies as plaintiffs advance legal theories aimed at holding platforms liable for harm to children. Here's a look at how the cases could be an early test of how courts may handle вҖӢsimilar claims going forward.

item.title: Jefferies profit rises on dealmaking but misses estimates from losses on First Brands, MFS
item.description: Jefferies Financial missed analysts' estimates on Wednesday after its profit jumped 22% in a first quarter that was buoyed by investment banking, вҖӢbut marred by losses on loans to collapsed companies.

item.title: Travel tech firm Navan sees strong 2027 revenue on demand from new customers
item.description: Corporate travel booking agency Navan on Wednesday forecast 2027 revenue above Wall Street estimates, banking on strong вҖӢdemand from on-boarding new company clients to its platform.

item.title: GM workers at Mexico's Silao plant to vote on proposal for 10% salary hike
item.description: The union вҖӢrepresenting workers at вҖҢa General Motors plant in Silao, вҖӢMexico has вҖӢproposed a 10% salary вҒ increase for вҖӢthe 2026-2028 contract, вҖӢunion leader told Reuters on Wednesday.

item.title: KKR-backed OHB taps banks for share sale, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: German satellite maker OHB is вҖӢworking on a share sale, potentially allowing minority вҖҢinvestor KKR to reduce its stake, Bloomberg News reported on Wednesday.

item.title: Fidelity beats lawsuit over fees in $439 billion money market fund
item.description: A federal judge dismissed a lawsuit on Wednesday accusing mutual вҖҢfund giant Fidelity of cheating many investors in its $439.1 billion Fidelity Government Money Market Fund by keeping them in higher-cost share classes than they were eligible for.

item.title: Private credit fund bonds were flagging risks before recent redemptions, hedge fund says
item.description: Bonds issued by semi-liquid private credit funds have fallen sharply in value вҖӢsince early February to trade at their weakest in a year, in a sign that investors were bracing вҖҢfor stress in the sector even before a rush of recent redemptions, according to bond trading hedge fund Fourier Asset Management.

item.title: What did jury decide in social media case against Meta and Google?
item.description: A Los Angeles jury found Alphabet's Google and Meta liable on Wednesday in a landmark вҖӢyouth social media addiction trial.

item.title: KKR to acquire Nothing Bundt Cakes for over $2 billion, WSJ reports
item.description: U.S. private вҖӢequity firm вҖҢKKR has struck вҖӢa вҖӢdeal to acquire вҒ bakery вҖӢchain вҖӢNothing Bundt Cakes from вҖӢRoark вҖӢCapital for over $2 вҖҢbillion, вҒ including debt, the Wall вҖӢStreet вҖӢJournal вҒ reported on вҖӢWednesday.

item.title: Thyssenkrupp, Jindal steel sale talks falter on pension, energy costs, sources say
item.description: Discussions of a possible sale of Thyssenkrupp's steel unit to Jindal Steel International could be called off due to differences over pension liabilities, вҖӢinvestments and energy costs, four people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Infosys to acquire US firms Optimum Healthcare IT for $465 million, Stratus for $95 million
item.description: Indian IT services firm вҖӢInfosys said on вҖҢWednesday it had entered into вҖӢseparate agreements вҖӢto acquire U.S.-based вҒ technology companies вҖӢOptimum Healthcare IT вҖӢand Stratus in all-cash deals.

item.title: JetBlue taps advisers for potential sale, Semafor reports
item.description: JetBlue Airways has brought in advisers to assess the viability of a sale to a rival carrier, Semafor reported on Wednesday, citing вҖӢpeople familiar with the matter.

item.title: US suspends anti-smog fuel rules in bid to ease pump prices
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration announced on Wednesday it will temporarily suspend federal anti-smog вҖӢregulations on seasonal gasoline blends to combat higher pump prices вҖҢsince the start of the war on Iran.

item.title: Jury finds Meta and Google liable in social media addiction trial
item.description: A Los Angeles jury found Alphabet's вҖҢGoogle and Meta liable on Wednesday in a landmark social media addiction lawsuit.
